seoTitle: How to Favorite a Page in Microsoft Loop (2026)
metaDescription: Bookmark important Microsoft Loop pages using the Favorites system so you can jump straight to them instead of digging through nested folders.
intro: Hunting through nested workspace trees to find the same important page every day slows you down. This guide covers how to star a page as a favorite in Microsoft Loop, find your bookmarks in the dedicated Favorites tab, and clean up old shortcuts you no longer need.

steps.heading: Open your workspace sidebar
steps.body: Navigate to your target Microsoft Loop workspace and open the left sidebar content menu.

steps.heading: Find the page to bookmark
steps.body: Hover over the specific page title you want to pin to reveal its inline context menu options.

steps.heading: Add it to favorites
steps.body: Click the three dots icon next to the title and select 'Add to Favorites' to instantly pin the page.

steps.heading: Access your favorites from the home tab
steps.body: Click the Loop logo to return to your central dashboard and open the dedicated Favorites tab to see all your bookmarked pages.

steps.heading: Manage your shortcuts
steps.body: Review your fast-access shortcuts periodically and click the star again on any page to remove it once it's no longer a priority.

faq.question: Do favorited pages sync across devices?
faq.answer: Yes, favorites are tied to your account and sync across web, mobile, and desktop versions of Loop.

faq.question: Can I favorite a sub-page, not just a top-level page?
faq.answer: Yes, you can bookmark sub-pages individually, which is useful for jumping straight to a specific section without navigating the full tree.

faq.question: How do I remove a page from my favorites?
faq.answer: Click the star icon again on the page, either from the sidebar or the Favorites tab, to unstar it.

faq.question: Is there a limit to how many pages I can favorite?
faq.answer: The description doesn't specify a hard limit, but keeping your favorites list focused on truly high-priority pages keeps it useful for fast navigation.
